item.title: About
item.description: This Site Hello there! Welcome to my brain's dumping ground. The inspiration to create a personal website came from reading through the monthly discussions on Hacker News about the advantages of doing so. Also, I like reading security blogs and would like to post my own research findings here while practicing my technical writing. That said, I want to use this site, not as a professional portfolio, but more as a means to organize and share my knowledge, ideas, thoughts, and resources.

item.title: Books
item.description: Current/Upcoming Reads Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig Remembering The Kanji 3 - James W. Heisig Neuromancer - William Gibson Count Zero - William Gibson Mona Lisa Overdrive - William Gibson Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders Backlog Security The Hardware Hacker - Andrew “bunnie” Huang Rootkits and Bootkits - Alex Matrosov, Eugene Rodionov, and Sergey Bratus Practical Binary Analysis - Dennis Andriesse Real-World Bug Hunting - Peter Yaworski Black Hat Python - Justin Seitz Attacking Network Protocols - James Forshaw The Hacker Playbook - Peter Kim Serious Cryptography - Jean-Philippe Aumasson Game Hacking - Nick Cano PoC||GTFO - Manul Laphroaig Novels Snow Crash - Neal Stephenson Pattern Recognition - William Gibson Recommendations Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

item.title: Goals
item.description: Interests AI / ML Agriculture Drawing Go (board game) Hardware Hiking Language Learning Piano Reading Reverse Engineering Security Space Starcraft Tea Travel Action Items November 2019 finish Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig continue A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim continue Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig to reach 2000 kanji re-read Neuromancer - William Gibson 40x situps, pushups, squats per day hiking once per week starcraft 1hr after work per weekday build a personal website December 2019 nothing!

item.title: Malware
item.description: Learning Courses hasherezade's guide - guide on how to start with malware analysis Open Security Training - mostly RE courses Lena's Reversing For Newbies - RE course Dr. Fu's Malware Analysis - RE course RPISEC's Malware Analysis - RE course based on Practical Malware Analysis SANS FOR610 for GREM - expensive RE cert Challenges ReversingHero - RE challenge program challenges.re - RE challenges by creator of beginners.re MalwareTech's Beginner Challenges - RE challenges by MalwareTech crackmes.

item.title: Misc
item.description: Goals Books Japanese Tea Starcraft Malware Pentesting Data Science Project Ideas

item.title: Pentesting
item.description: Learning Online Tulpa's prep guide for OSCP TJnull's prep guide for OSCP Books Penetration Testing - Georgia Weidman Hacking: The Art of Exploitation - Jon Erickson Real-World Bug Hunting - Peter Yaworski Black Hat Python - Justin Seitz Attacking Network Protocols - James Forshaw The Hacker Playbook - Peter Kim Metasploit - David Kennedy, Jim O’Gorman, Devon Kearns, and Mati Aharoni Tools CTFs CTFtime - CTF tracker

item.title: Project Ideas
item.description: Atom extension or python script for defanging or parsing and refanging URLs and IPs in IoCs Automate countermeasure creation from blog post IoCs; “blogtoyara” yara engine running free + my rules and GUI visualization Threat hunting playbook definitions (incl. enumeration of normal, easy wins) IR playbook definitions (incl. scoping questions, first steps) Threat group profiler pulling from free online TI streams
